title: Mailroom
summary: A local-first email client proving real data-router fetchers, navigation blockers, durable drafts, and automatic offline outbox delivery.

journeys.title: Open a message deep link, navigate by keyboard, and persist a fetcher-owned star without navigating
Navigate every compact mailbox link by accessible name and keyboard without overflowing the mobile page
Block a dirty draft navigation, restore editor focus, save through a fetcher, and leave safely
Reset the editor across draft routes and update one adopted draft identity across repeated saves
Queue a message offline, retain it across route remounts, and deliver it automatically after reconnection
Retry a deterministic loader failure and distinguish empty and missing mailbox states
Keep a slow fetcher mutation alive across navigation and recover a rejected online send

faultScenarios.description: The fault=load query rejects the first inbox loader for a session and succeeds on explicit revalidation.
The fault=slow-mutation query keeps a shell-mounted shared fetcher active while the user navigates to another mailbox.
The fault=send query rejects the first online send for a session while retaining every draft field for explicit retry.
Native offline state stores a complete queued message locally, and the online event drives automatic fetcher delivery.
